claims.fieldOrBehavior: All four accepted criteria pass. ac1-contract-doc: context/contracts/trigger-guards.md requires dedup/cooldown/daily-cap/concurrency-lock declarations with defaults and binds #252/#250 to the contract. ac2-validation-warning: parseKitAgentSpawnTriggers warns (never errors) on a guardless spawning surface; kit install surfaces it non-blockingly; unit tests cover warning, shape errors, and channel separation. ac3-conforming-examples: plumb#1 and ephemeris cited (citation-only, accepted gap G1). ac4-suite-integrity: kits/builder still validates clean; errors-only validateKitRepository signature preserved; build and static suite green.

claims.fieldOrBehavior: Manifest-lane integration proof: the kit install/merge suite (87 checks) passes on the delivered change, exercising the kit CLI install machinery that now runs validateKitRepositoryDiagnostics and prints guard warnings on every install. The direct behavioral proof for the new declaration parsing, warning channel, and both-install-source warning emission is the extended kit-metadata-security unit suite (37/37 via node --test), which CI reconciles through its own fresh trust-reconcile-verify static run (test_unit_helpers lane); it is not recorded as a separate labeled command here because it is not an individually reconcilable manifest lane.

artifact_refs.summary: Exact substantive manifest integration command recorded and executed by the canonical writer for this verification result.

claims.fieldOrBehavior: Evidence confidence PASS: acceptance evidence maps to the intended five-file scope with no unexplained drift; strongest-class evidence (37/37 unit tests incl. both-install-forms CLI test, 87/87 install-merge manifest lane, live CLI warning observation) plus reproducible canonical commands (npm run build, npm run eval:static both exit 0); independent distinct-actor critique iteration 1 FAIL routed back and fixed, iteration 2 PASS on both lanes; residual items are disclosed accepted gaps (plumb#1/ephemeris citation-only; no runtime enforcement by design; two session-env-only integration lanes proven environmental on a pristine origin/main baseline; one stale line pointer in the recorded ac4 source ref, corrected in this report). Merge-review confidence only — not release or deployment authorization; publish is the orchestrator's.

artifact_refs.summary: Evidence confidence, criterion coverage, scope-integrity decision, disclosed corrections, and residual risks.

claims.fieldOrBehavior: A standing contract doc exists in context/contracts/ requiring that any kit, hook, or integration spawning an agent run from a trigger declares, with defaults: dedup by trigger/failure signature, cooldown per subject, daily cap, and a concurrency lock; it notes that any implementation of kontourai/flow-agents#252 (background-review fork, autonomy epic #250) must declare its guards.

criterion.description: A standing contract doc exists in context/contracts/ requiring that any kit, hook, or integration spawning an agent run from a trigger declares, with defaults: dedup by trigger/failure signature, cooldown per subject, daily cap, and a concurrency lock; it notes that any implementation of kontourai/flow-agents#252 (background-review fork, autonomy epic #250) must declare its guards.

evidence_refs.excerpt: ## Required Guard Declarations — dedup_key / cooldown_seconds / daily_cap / max_concurrent table with defaults
evidence_refs.summary: All four guard declarations with defaults; §Future Consumers binds #252/#250.

claims.fieldOrBehavior: kit.json supports an optional, additive agent_spawn_triggers declaration; kit repository validation emits a WARNING (never an error) when an entry with spawns_agent_runs=true lacks complete guard config (dedup_key, cooldown_seconds, daily_cap, max_concurrent), and the kit CLI surfaces that warning non-blockingly.

criterion.description: kit.json supports an optional, additive agent_spawn_triggers declaration; kit repository validation emits a WARNING (never an error) when an entry with spawns_agent_runs=true lacks complete guard config (dedup_key, cooldown_seconds, daily_cap, max_concurrent), and the kit CLI surfaces that warning non-blockingly.

Manifest-lane integration proof: the kit install/merge suite (87 checks) passes on the delivered change, exercising the kit CLI install machinery that now runs validateKitRepositoryDiagnostics and prints guard warnings on every install. The direct behavioral proof for the new declaration parsing, warning channel, and both-install-source warning emission is the extended kit-metadata-security unit suite (37/37 via node --test), which CI reconciles through its own fresh trust-reconcile-verify static run (test_unit_helpers lane); it is not recorded as a separate labeled command here because it is not an individually reconcilable manifest lane.
